name: Early dark energy (EDE)
summary: An ultralight scalar field with mass m_phi ~ 10^-27 eV that briefly behaves as a cosmological constant near matter-radiation equality (z ~ 3500) before decaying away as ~ a^-4 or faster. The brief EDE-domination episode shrinks the sound horizon at recombination, raising the inferred H_0 from CMB data and reducing the Hubble tension from 5 sigma to ~3 sigma. Leading early-universe-side resolution of the H0 tension.

addresses_puzzles.note: EDE with fraction f_EDE ~ 10% at z_critical ~ 3500 lifts H_0(CMB) from 67.4 to ~72-73, matching SH0ES. Trade-off: introduces 2-3 sigma worsening of the S8 tension (LSS clusters less than Planck-LambdaCDM predicts). Not a clean solution but the leading active proposal.
Late-time Lambda problem unchanged. EDE adds a separate early-universe vacuum-energy episode that decays, suggesting multiple cosmologically-acting scalars rather than one Lambda.

touches_benchmarks.note: Modifies the acoustic-peak structure subtly; Planck + ACT + SPT can detect or exclude specific EDE potentials. Hill et al. (2020, arXiv:2003.07355) showed EDE consistent with Planck-alone but pressured by S8.
Worsens S8 tension by ~2-3 sigma; full-shape galaxy power spectrum (BOSS, DESI) is the discriminating test.

introduces.note: Mass m_phi, fraction f_EDE, critical redshift z_c (often parameterised through initial field value and potential shape).

history: Poulin, Smith, Karwal, Kamionkowski (2018), 'Early dark energy can resolve the Hubble tension', Phys. Rev. Lett. 122, 221301 (2019), arXiv:1811.04083. Subsequent model-building and constraints: Smith et al. 2020, Hill et al. 2020 (arXiv:2003.07355), Murgia et al. 2021. Among the leading H_0-tension resolutions as of 2024.

references.citation: Poulin, Smith, Karwal, Kamionkowski, 'Early dark energy can resolve the Hubble tension', Phys. Rev. Lett. 122, 221301 (2019)
